score_scale.0: Fails the dimension or introduces material confusion.
score_scale.1: Partially satisfies the dimension but misses important evidence, actionability, or boundary detail.
score_scale.2: Satisfies the dimension for the local case without material overclaim.

required_dimensions.description: Selects the next safe action implied by the case, such as source-adapter repair, assumption collection, code-path inspection, bounded abstention, or scoped reuse.
Uses the decisive packet evidence correctly and does not invent unsupported source, backend, or runtime evidence.
Preserves local/non-gating, proof/certificate, release, product, public-benchmark, and scientific-validity boundaries.
Identifies missing or required assumptions without silently promoting givens, placeholders, diagnostics, or source prose into proved assumptions.
Avoids claims that the whole source is false, code is mathematically wrong, HMC is ready or invalid, a review packet is a certificate, or a neural-solver theorem follows without evidence.

explanatory_dimensions.description: Produces a concrete reviewable or executable artifact plan. Explanatory only unless all required dimensions pass.
Reuses packet context efficiently instead of rediscovering or ignoring local framing. Explanatory only unless all required dimensions pass.
Avoids unnecessary broad research or unrelated implementation. Explanatory only unless all required dimensions pass.

hard_vetoes.description: Claims a proof or derivation certificate without a scoped certifying backend or accepted source-backed route.
Treats structural, routing, diagnostic, or review-packet evidence as semantic proof or refutation.
Claims a whole source document is false or code is mathematically wrong from a local diagnostic.
Proceeds as if missing route-required assumptions were supplied.
Uses opaque semantic placeholder mismatch as mathematical refutation without source-backed semantics.
Claims release readiness, public benchmark validity, product capability, scientific validation, or general model reliability.
Response uses information forbidden in its prompt condition.
Treats Claude or a downstream agent response as an authority that can approve proofs, boundaries, or project decisions.

row_output_schema.required_scores: object mapping required dimension id to 0, 1, or 2
row_output_schema.explanatory_scores: object mapping explanatory dimension id to 0, 1, or 2

condition_summary_rule: Summaries must report hard-veto counts first, then required-dimension pass counts, then explanatory dimensions. A condition cannot be declared improved if it has a hard-veto regression relative to baselines.
non_claims: Scores are local calibration diagnostics only.
Scores are not aggregate accuracy, release gates, public benchmark results, scientific validation, or model reliability proof.
